business_angle: Steel City Electrics are the Sheffield team (the lads) who take on the heavy work other electricians put off: full house re-wires and tired or burnt-out consumer units stripped out and modernised, then tested and left safe and working before they go - punctual, transparent on price, and quick to answer.
why_this_trader_wins: The reviews point at one specialism the rest of the trade tends to avoid: heavy-current re-wiring and board modernisation. Andy had a whole house re-wired ('full house re-wiring on a house of mine ... efficient and very reasonably priced'); Alina describes the safety-first finish ('made sure everything was safe and working perfectly before leaving'); Wakas, used them more than once, calls them 'always punctual and transparent ... thanks again lads'; Pat, an older customer on her own, 'felt safe at all times' and was quoted 'a very competitive' price. That is a 5.0 from 20 reviews with no complaint to soften - rewiring done properly, explained, and made safe.
target_customer: Sheffield homeowners and landlords (the S postcodes, from Highfield and Heeley out across the city) who need a full or partial re-wire, an old or failing fuse board replaced with a modern consumer unit, or an electrical fault traced and made safe - and who want a clear price and a straight answer.
page_single_job: Get that Sheffield homeowner or landlord to request a quote (or call) for a re-wire, consumer-unit replacement, or fault-finding job.

customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Andy): 'came to do a full house re-wiring on a house of mine. Fantastic service from start to finish. They were efficient and very reasonably priced'

customer_praise_themes.theme: left safe and working, checked before leaving
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[1] (Alina): 'completed the work efficiently and made sure everything was safe and working perfectly before leaving'; reviews[0] (Pat): 'happy for a call back if anything was not quite right'
brief.json reviews[3] (Wakas): 'Always punctual and tranparent'; reviews[0] (Pat): 'the price quoted was very competitive'

customer_praise_themes.theme: quick to respond, easy to deal with
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[0] (Pat): 'always got a quick response to my texts ... Did not have to wait long for the jobs to be done'; reviews[4] (Samaira): 'Very easy to communicate with and helped with all my electrical issues'

customer_praise_themes.theme: explains the work clearly, customers feel safe
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[1] (Alina): 'He explained everything clearly'; reviews[0] (Pat): 'As an older lady on my own, I felt safe at all times'

customer_praise_themes.theme: a team / the lads, used more than once
customer_praise_themes.evidence: brief.json reviews[2] (Andy): 'These guys came to do'; reviews[3] (Wakas): 'Had steel city out multiple times ... Thanks again lads'

named_people.basis: brief.json owner_name=null, contact_name=null, contact_name_usage_allowed=false. The only candidate, 'Did', is a one-review fragment (almost certainly the start of 'Did not have to wait long' in Pat's review, misread as a name) and is explicitly flagged 'not used in copy'. The reviews refer to the business as a team ('these guys', 'the lads', 'Had steel city out'). Copy uses the team framing ('the lads', 'the Steel City team', 'we') and the business name 'Steel City Electrics', never a personal first name. Some reviews say 'he' (a single attendee), but with no name evidenced the site stays on the team.

strongest_review_quote.text: These guys came to do a full house re-wiring on a house of mine. Fantastic service from start to finish. They were efficient and very reasonably priced. I will definitely be using them again in the future. Highly recommend.
strongest_review_quote.author: Andy

claims_to_avoid: ANY certification or membership wording: NICEIC, NAPIT, Part P, 'registered', 'approved', 'certified electrician', 'qualified electrician', 'fully insured' - NONE are verified (brief.json directory_probes: 0 verified, all NOT_FOUND/HOMONYM/snippet-only with empty badges; brief.certifications absent). This is the central electrician trap: do not imply a scheme membership the evidence does not carry. (An EICR / electrical safety CERTIFICATE issued for a job is a normal deliverable and may be referenced as a document the customer receives, but NOT as a trade-body accreditation of the business.)
Trustpilot / Yell / MyBuilder as proof - all snippet-only, single-signal, unverified (brief.json directory_probes)
a specific years-trading number - none evidenced
any personal first name - owner_name=null; 'Did' is a misread review fragment, flagged not-for-use

literal '24/7' / 'open 24 hours' claims as a headline promise - the Places hours are an unreliable artifact (all 7 days 'Open 24 hours'); frame availability softly as 'quick to answer' / 'call-outs', anchored to Pat's 'quick response' and the listed 'Emergency callouts' service, not a literal round-the-clock guarantee
the street address '50 Fieldhead Rd' / 'Highfield' exact street anywhere public - use Sheffield / S8 only
service areas beyond Sheffield and its S postcodes (one-van/small-team radius); no regional or national footprint

the navy + red Steel City logo image (03/08 in the gallery): it is an AI-style brand asset whose palette (navy + red) conflicts with the mandated monochrome-industrial direction - do not place it on the page
describing the burnt-board photos with alarm or as a scare tactic; caption them plainly as fault/board work in Sheffield
personality: straight-talking, capable, unflustered; a Sheffield team that takes the heavy electrical job, prices it honestly, explains what it is doing, and leaves it tested and safe
evidence_strength: moderate-content / single-source (5.0 from 20 specific Google reviews + 6 Google Places photos that are fault/board shots, not finished-room showcases; no verified third-party profile - directory/Facebook unverified)
pitch_hook_summary: Lead on the re-wire-and-make-safe angle (full re-wires, old/burnt boards modernised, faults traced and left tested), the Sheffield steel-city identity, and a clean 5.0/20 Google.

metadata.manual_review_note: Single-source (Google-only): no verified third-party profile and contactability=NEEDS_MANUAL_REVIEW (no email, UK mobile only; WhatsApp manual per ARCH-5). This is a BUILD run; pitch_gate enforces at outreach time, not build time. The evidence is specific and usable - same posture as the Kyle Knowles reference build (also source_quality=FAIL). Clean target: truly_no_website=true, HAS_REAL_SITE=false.
metadata.authored_blind: Path B authored from frozen evidence + CLAUDE.md + the webfortrades-site-design skill only. Pre-allocated design seed (parallel batch): palette family = monochrome-industrial, body = Public Sans, display category = sans-condensed. None of the 5 reserved specific palettes or 3 reserved body fonts is used. See design-direction.md for the explicit distinctness check.
